data.epi_content:
Every time I pass by, everyone glances at me.

I can't walk properly, nor do I look presentable.

This isn't me.

This isn't Erica Mecklenburg.

Even if my arms and legs were broken, I should walk with my back straight, always maintain proper etiquette whether speaking to a mortal enemy or a close friend, and ensure that if someone disrespects me first, they pay the appropriate price. But I can't do that.

So this isn't me.

I'm just something that slightly resembles the appearance of someone who was once called a noble, now fallen.

Then who am I?

My head, body, and way of thinking are all tangled up, living in contradictions.

What I used to like has completely disappeared from my memory, and even my food preferences are changing.

I barely managed to walk through the corridor to reach my room.

The smell of sweat, a hint of flesh, and a foul odor rise up.

I struggled into the room, stripped off all my clothes, turned on the water in the bathtub, and quickly washed my body.

"This isn't about finding myself, what is this?"

My distinctive body odor, or rather, various foul smells including sweat, rise up as they meet the hot water.

I had been sweating so much that my clothes were soaked.

I suddenly looked at my reflection in the mirror.

When I lift the corners of my mouth, I smile.

So, this is me.

At least when no one else is around, I can do whatever I want with my body.

"What do I mean it's not me?

I should just accept that this is who I am now."

After cleaning my body, I step into the bathtub.

It was so hot I thought my skin might cook.

A slight moan-like sound escapes from my mouth.

"I'm hungry."

Dripping water, I get out of the bathtub and grab a few chocolates.

Then suddenly remembering the pistol—specifically, how I had wanted to blow my head off in the bathtub—I take it out from the drawer and place it on the table.

Since it has no bullets, I insert two bullets while completely naked in the cold air, then spin the chamber so I can't tell where they are.

"If I mess up, I won't even get to eat chocolate... does it matter?"

I put it against my temple, click.

No bullet fired.

I used to be so scared when pulling the trigger.

Now I feel somewhat indifferent, thinking I might go back if I die.

It was only once, but I've somehow gotten used to it.

I return to the bathroom.

Thanks to my head not being blown off, I can eat chocolate.

I stuff my mouth with chocolate and sink back into the bathtub.

"Somehow, being in water is the most comfortable."

Why am I so obsessed with bathing?

Perhaps it's because of the unpleasant thoughts in my head.

I'm washing away negative thoughts about death by letting the water flow over me.

Even if I ramble on in my head, there's no one to listen.

Ah, I suppose it's the same even if I speak out loud—there's no one to listen.
data.epi_content2:
"I should cut down on talking to myself."

Since I've been alone for so long with no one to talk to, I've started muttering to myself more frequently.

Knock. Knock.

While thinking about this, my body slumped in the bathtub, I hear someone knocking on the door.

Too lazy to move, I just stay lying there.

Then the knocking becomes stronger, repeated several times.

After a while, I hear the sound of something breaking and the door opening.

"Erica! Where are you?!"

It was Vivian.

Too lazy to answer, I don't respond.

I asked to be left alone.

She doesn't listen to me at all.

Well, I've asked her many things—don't follow me, don't worry about me, don't be with Evan and me, don't pretend to be friendly with me, don't think of me as a friend, don't touch my body—but Vivian hasn't listened to any of it.

She walks around the room for a while before finally opening the bathroom door.

Her face turns slightly red.

I am naked after all, but blushing at the sight of another woman's body seems a bit concerning.

"Vivian, do you enjoy breaking into other people's rooms and looking at their naked bodies?"

I don't bother twisting my body to cover myself or blush in embarrassment.

I'm a bit embarrassed, but not enough to make a fuss.

"Uh, ah, um, I thought something had happened, just... I'm sorry!"

"This is embarrassing. But I won't say anything about it."

After lying in the bathtub with my muscles relaxed, I realize I should do something since I have a guest, so I get up.

My legs are still trembling slightly, but I can manage.

I just need to sit and talk, then send her away at an appropriate time.

I put on fresh clothes I had prepared inside, look in the mirror to fix my hair, and dry it.

When I come out of the bathroom, Vivian isn't sitting in a chair but standing with her back against the wall.

"Why are you standing there? Sit in the chair."

At my words, Vivian neatly sits in the chair next to the table.

She's holding a small bag in her hand, which she places on the table.

"Um... these are cookies I baked this morning. Would you like some?"

My throat hurts, so instead of answering, I just nod.

Vivian's face brightens with a fresh smile.

They're chocolate-flavored cookies, similar to the chocolate I've been eating too much of, but with a different texture.

While watching me eat the cookies, Vivian hesitates a bit before asking a question.

"Why were you locked in there?"

"Because someone else forcibly locked me in, that's why I was trapped."

Hearing this, Vivian looks slightly stunned.

Of course, it's hard to imagine.

Until recently, I was a confident young lady leading others around, so it's unimaginable why I'm suddenly being treated this way.

As Vivian looks at me with confused emotions and a hint of pity in her eyes, what little self-esteem I thought I had left crumbles completely.
data.epi_content3:
And that's all this young lady had.

Useless stubbornness and obstinacy, and an attachment to the life she had lived so far.

More precisely, an attachment not to being alive, but to a way of living.

That's why both this young lady and I are broken somewhere.

When the way of living, what might be called daily life, collapses, one becomes nothing.

Most people, when faced with such a situation, despair and fall apart.

And given the slightest excuse, they seek what is commonly called an extreme choice.

I just want to die beautifully alone, even if I die.

To feel not ugly when I look at myself, compared to others.

But death doesn't seem as beautiful as it appears in fancy phrases and dramatic novels.

How could I deserve such a thing when I can't endure even a little pain or control my emotions?

So was it because I wasn't worthy?

Because I don't deserve to die, am I sent back to this time?

Who, why, for what purpose, reason, reason, reason.

I shift words around in my head trying to figure it out.

There's nothing.

Ah, have I been lost in thought for too long?

Vivian looks at me with a mixture of discomfort and concern.

I feel like I'm starting to get used to that look.

"Vivian, I was just having a difficult time earlier.

I'm fine now, so you don't need to look at me like that."

"Yes, I believe you!"

"It's not about believing, it's just how it is."

Come to think of it, where did the pistol I put on the table go?

Does it matter?

"By the way, Vivian, we're not the kind of people who visit each other's rooms like this."

"If you were me, what would you think if you saw someone who was just trapped in a locker, then escaped looking somewhat deranged before returning to their room? Especially if they looked extremely anxious!"

"Are you worried?"

I ask in a mocking tone, crossing my legs and looking slightly arrogant.

But Vivian, ignoring my tone, answers firmly with a serious expression.

"Yes."

"I'm fine."

When I say this, Vivian slightly furrows her brow.

Or maybe she looks like she might cry.

Her face is unnecessarily beautiful, making it hard to gauge her emotions.

"And Vivian, we're not the kind of people who worry about each other and check on each other.

I said before... no, I didn't. Anyway, that's how it is."

Vivian doesn't answer and just continues sitting in front of me with her mouth closed.

Neither of us initiates conversation, creating a slightly irritating silence.

So I just ignore Vivian and do what I normally do—brew coffee and start eating chocolate.

I realize that even with an uncomfortable person in the room, I can feel some satisfaction when my mind clears a bit and sweetness and bitterness simultaneously fill my mouth.

After eating five or six chocolates, Vivian finally speaks.

"...What was the pistol on the table for?"
